channel.title: Semantic-Incorrectness on Team Atlanta
channel.description: Recent content in Semantic-Incorrectness on Team Atlanta

item.title: Patch Generation in 2026: Evaluating Coding Agents on AIxCC Final Competition Challenges
item.description:
LLM-based patch generation has become a practical approach to fixing software vulnerabilities. Tools like Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini can read code, reason about bugs, and produce patches — often in seconds. But how good are they, really?

In this post, we evaluate ten coding agent configurations on their ability to autonomously generate security patches for real-world vulnerabilities from the DARPA AIxCC final competition. We present the overall results, and then focus on the most subtle and dangerous failure mode: semantic incorrectness — patches that look plausible, compile, and pass tests, but do not actually fix the vulnerability.
